dimensions.rationale: There is no conversation transcript provided to evaluate. With zero coach turns, the coach cannot demonstrate avoidance of anti-patterns in any positive sense, but more importantly there is no evidence of the coaching behaviors required to score above 1. Per the rubric, scoring requires assessing what the coach actually did; an empty transcript cannot earn a 3 (which requires demonstrated avoidance across a full conversation) or a 2 (which requires a mild slip with otherwise intact coaching). Defaulting to 1 reflects that the dimension cannot be affirmatively satisfied.
dimensions.notes: Conversation body is empty in the prompt. If this is a data error rather than an actual empty session, rescoring against the real transcript is warranted.

dimensions.rationale: There is no conversation to evaluate — the transcript is empty. With no coach utterances, none of the VIEW components (vulnerability, impartiality, empathy, wonder) can be observed as present. By the rubric, absence of demonstrated VIEW defaults to a poor score; there is no evidence of genuine curiosity, undefended presence, or matched pace because there is no coaching at all.
dimensions.notes: Empty transcript provided. Score reflects absence of any demonstrated coaching stance, not an active anti-pattern.

dimensions.rationale: No conversation transcript was provided to evaluate. With zero client turns available, there is no evidence of any shift in language, somatic state, or stance from start to end. The breakthrough condition (client softening, locating the want underneath, recognizing 'selfish' as an inherited label) cannot be demonstrated as met without client text to cite. Per the scoring rubric, absence of visible shift in client turns = score 1.
dimensions.notes: Conversation body was empty in the prompt. If this is an error, rescoring would require the actual transcript. As submitted, there is no client movement to score.

dimensions.rationale: There is no conversation to evaluate — the transcript is empty. With zero coach moves, there is no evidence of move-to-read matching, sequence integrity, or timing. By the rubric, absence of any AoA-specific moves and absence of any deployment defaults to the lowest score.
dimensions.notes: No conversation content was provided to score.

dimensions.rationale: There is no conversation provided to evaluate. With zero coach turns, there are no signature questions, no framework invocations, no distinction language, and no AoA-specific moves to credit. By default this cannot rise above a 1 on methodology fidelity, since the methodology was not deployed at all (whether appropriately or not).
dimensions.notes: Conversation transcript was empty in the prompt. Score reflects absence of any methodology deployment.

dimensions.rationale: There is no conversation to evaluate — the transcript is empty. With no coach turns visible, there are no reads to score. Per the rubric, a coach who never names how the client is showing up scores 1; since no reads can be detected in text, the dimension defaults to the lowest score.
dimensions.notes: Conversation body is empty in the provided input. Scoring reflects absence of any visible reads.
